name: NH Birth to Three: Family Centered Early Supports & Services
description: New Hampshire's early intervention program providing coaching and support for families of infants and toddlers (birth to 3) with developmental delays, established conditions, or at risk of delay. Services are delivered in natural settings like homes and childcare through 10 regional sites statewide.

name: The Arc of New Hampshire
description: Supporting citizens with intellectual and developmental disabilities since 1954 in New Hampshire. Promotes and protects the human rights of people with IDD, actively supporting their full inclusion and participation in the community.

name: Special Olympics New Hampshire
description: Provides year-round sports training and athletic competition for over 3,000 children and adults with intellectual disabilities, offering continuing opportunities to develop physical fitness, demonstrate courage, and build friendships through sport.

name: Disability Rights Center - NH
description: New Hampshire's designated Protection and Advocacy agency for over 40 years. Protects, advances, and strengthens the legal rights of all people with disabilities through information, referral, advice, legal representation, investigations, and policy reform.

name: VR New Hampshire (Bureau of Vocational Rehabilitation)
description: Offers vocational rehabilitation services through seven regional offices to help individuals with disabilities prepare for, obtain, retain, and advance in employment. Services range from job training and placement to school transition and independent living support.
ageRange: Working age adults and transition-age youth

name: NAMI New Hampshire
description: Grassroots organization improving the lives of people affected by mental illness and suicide through support, education, and advocacy. Provides an Information and Resource Line, the NAMI HelpLine, affiliate chapters, and support groups statewide.

name: Assistive Technology in New Hampshire (ATinNH)
description: A project of the UNH Institute on Disability connecting people across New Hampshire with assistive technology through a lending library for short and long-term loans, demonstrations, training workshops, and reuse programs to expand access and independence.

name: NH ABLE Plan
description: New Hampshire's ABLE savings plan administered by the NH State Treasury, helping individuals with disabilities save while preserving SSI and Medicaid benefits. Offers 8 investment options including a checking account with debit card. Open an account with as little as $1.

name: Dartmouth Health Children's - Child Development Program
description: Provides diagnostic evaluations and follow-up care for children with developmental and behavioral disorders at Dartmouth Health Children's in Lebanon, NH. Multidisciplinary team includes developmental pediatricians, psychologists, and neuropsychologists serving a wide region across NH and VT.

name: New England Disabled Sports
description: Nationally recognized program providing year-round adaptive sport instruction to adults and children with physical and cognitive disabilities at Loon Mountain and Bretton Woods in New Hampshire. Offers skiing, snowboarding, and other seasonal adaptive sports programs.
